Celifornia
Fair P01itical
Practices Cornmission
January 2A, l99L
Bi 11 weednan
City Planner
527 Encinitas Boulevard
Encin j,tas, cA 92024
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. I-90-759
Dear Mr. weedman:
This letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding the duties and respons ibilities 
of city of Encinitas
etinning comrnissioner BiLl Dean under the conf I ict-of-interest
provisi5ns of the Political Reform Act (the "Act';.1 
we have
insufficient infornation to determine the irnpact of the pending
decisions upon litr. Dean's source of income. Accordingly, vre treat
your letter as a request for informal assistance under the
irovj.sions of Regul;tion 18329 (copy enclosed) .2
OUESTTON
Do the conf I ict-of- interest provisions of the Act disqualify
Planning comrnissioner BilI Dean from participating in decisions
regarding an apptication for a design review permit which has been
appealed to the planning commission?
CONCLUSION
San Dieguito National Bank has been a source of income to
Planning Co:nmissioger Bill Dean in excess of $250 in the preceding
twelve months. Accordingl-y, the conflict-of-interest 
provisions
of the Act disqualify Mr. Dean from participating in decisions
regarding an application for a design review pernit which wiII
1 co.r"rr,."nt code Sections 81ooo-91015. All statutory references
are to the covernnent code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations Section
18oo0, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to Title 
2
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the reguestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formaL written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329 (c) . )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO o P.O. Box 8o7 . sacramento cA 958o4-o8o7 o (916) 3 22-5660


File No. I-9 0-7 59
Page 2
have a foreseeable and material financial effect on
the bank.
rACTS
Planning commissioner Dean is the sole owner of Dean and
Associates, i public relations firn in Encinitas.3 San oieguito
National Bank i-s one of l1r. Dean's clients. 
Mr. Dean represented
the bank from January L9, L99O, to Septemher 30, 1990, and
received 9l-,800 for his services to the bank.
Mr. Lester Young owns two adjacent lots in the city of
Encinitas. These fots are located in a develoPed commercial area.
one of these lots is presently unimproved and Mr. Young is in the
process of obtaining the necessary permits to erect an office
building on this 1ot. !tr. Young has applied for a design review
permit fo obtain approval of the design of the projected office
bui.Iding. rhis application has been appealed to the planning
cornmission.
The adjacent 1ot is inproved with a building which is
currently leased to San oieguito National Bank.
You are the city planner for the city of Encinitas. 
Your
department reviews and- issues pernits to the public. 
The city
attorney for the city of Encinitas has reviewed this matter and
has requested that you seek our advice on behalf of I'lr. Dean who
has authorized your request for advice.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits Public officills 
from making,
participating in, oi using their official 
position to influence a
lovernrnental decision in which they know or have reason to knol
fhey have a financial interest. 
Planning conmissioners are pubLic
officials. 
(Section 82048. )
An official 
has a financial interest in a decision within the
ureaning of section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable - that the
decision will have a material financial effect, distingruishable
fron its effect on'the public generally, on the officiaf 
or on a
member of the officiaf i inroediate fanity,4 or on:
(c) Any source of incone, other than gifts
and other than loans by a comnercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
3 Th""" facts incorporate information you provided to ne during
our telephone conversation of January 3, 199L, and information
provided to ne by Mr. Dean during our telephone conversation of
January 7, 199L.
4 An official's 
" immediate fanilyrr are his or her spouse and
dependent children. 
(Section 82029 . )


Fi■e No. I-90-759
Page 3
terms avaifable to the public without regard to
officiaL status, aggregating tlro hundred fifty
dollars ($2so1 or more in value provided to,
received by or pronised to the pubtic official
within 12 months prior to the tine $rhen the
deci.sion is made.
Section 87103 (c) '
You have advised us that San Dieguito National Bank, which
has been a source of income in excess of $25o to Planning
Cornrnissioner Dean in the preceding twelve nonths, has a leasehold
interest in property situated imrnediately adjacent to the property
which is the- suLjecL of the pending decj.sions. Accordingly, if it
is reasonably foieseeable that the effect of the pending decisions
on san Diego National Bank will be material, Mr. Dean may not
participate in such decisions.
Foreseeabi l ity
The effects of a decision are reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantj-aI likelihood that they will occur. To be
foreseeable, the effects of a decision uust be more than a nere
possibilityl 
however certainty is not reguired. 
(DowneY Cares v.
bownev communitv Development Cornm. (1987) 196 cal.App.3d 983, 989-
s rI- 
* uoirow (L9771 70 caI.APP.3d 817,' rn re Thorner (1975)
1 FPPC Ops. 198 (copy enclosed). ) The Act seeks to prevent more
than actual conflicts of interest,' it seeks to prevent even the
appearance of a possible conflict of interest. 
(witt v. U.9.EE9!9,
supra at 823. )
we have insufficient facts to deternine the exact inpact of
the pending decisions on the bank which has been a source of
incorne to Mr. Dean. Holrever, it appears reasonabLy foreseeable
that the oroiect will interfere with the bank's use of its drive-
through wina5ws.5 lt is also foreseeabte that the business
tenants of the projected building will do business with the bank
thus increasing its revenues. !Ir. Dean also indicated to ne that
the bank is considering leasing space in the new building and that
5 Yo,, have submitted for our consideration a letter fron Thomas
J. Harron to Roger Krauel dated Decernber 5, 1990 (copy enclosed).
In thj-s letter, ttr. Harron states that the project under
consideration would interfere wj,th the bank's drive-through
operation and reduce the capacity of its drive-through lines. 
lilr.
Harron also states that ur. Paul Schedler, president of san
Dieguito National- Bank, indicated to hin that the project would
significantly 
decrease the bank's use or enj oyment of the leased
property. The conmission does not act as a finder of fact.
Hovrever, if Mr. Harron's letter is correct, those facts should be
considered when determining whether Mr. Dean may participate in
deci,sions regarding the perrnit application.


File No. I-9O-759
Page 4
the bank may also provide financing for the construction of the
projected Uuifaing. Taken as a whole, this infonnation leads us
Lo concLude that it is foreseeabte that decisions regarding the
pending design review pernit witl have a financial effect on the
Lanf wtrictr his been a source of income to Mr. Dean in the
preceding twelve months.
once it has been deternined that it is foreseeable that a
decision will have a financiat effect on a public official's
source of income, it must be deternined $rhether the effect will be
naterial.
Material-itv
when a decision rrilL have an indirect effect on a business
entity which has been a source of income to a public offi'cial, 
the
approlriate standard for determining materiality is that-of
negufition ].a702.2 (copy enclosed). If decisions regarding the
penaing permit application wilt affect the bank in the sums stated
in thii iegulation, ltr. Dean nust disqualify himself from
participating in such decisions.
For exampJ.e, if the bank is not listed on the Ner', York stock
Exchange or the American Stock Exchange but is listed on the
Nation;1 Association of Securities Dealers National Market List,
the appropriate standard for deternining nateriality is that of
Sufdivisiirn (b) of Regnrlation L87O2.2 rrhich states that a decision
is rnaterial if:
(1) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease in the gross revenues for- a fiscal year
of $15o,ooo or more; or
(2) The decision will result in the business
entity incurring or avoiding additional expenses or
reducing or eliminating existing expenses for a
fiscal year in the amount of $50,000 or more; or
(3) The decision will resutt in an increase
or decrease in the value of assets or liabilities
of 9150,0o0 or nore.
Regulation 787 02.2 (b) .
The Act precludes participation in decisions which will have
a material financial effect on a public official's 
financial
interests at the time the decision is made. Thus, a disgualifying
financial interest exists if the inpact of the decision is
foreseeable and material at the tiroe the decision is rnade.
Whereas the financial effect of a decision may not always be
easily assessed, it is incunbent upon the public officiat 
rrho has
a financial interest to rnake a qood faith effort to deternine, to


File No. I-90-759
Page 5
the best of his or her ability, 
the effect of the decision on the
financial interest. 
Accordingly, ur. Dean must make a good faith
effort to determine the effect of the planning decisions upon the
bank. If it is deternined that the effect of the decisions is
material, llr. Dean must abstain from partici.pating in such
decisions.
we trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Shoutd you have any further guestions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call ne at (916) 322-5907.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General counsel

By: Blanca u. Breeze
counsel, Legal Division
